info.name: RocketDelivery Restaurant API - Schema v2.0 CRUD Testing
info.description:
Complete CRUD testing collection for RocketDelivery Restaurant REST API

⚠️ SCHEMA v2.0 UPDATE (March 27, 2026):
- Restaurants now require addressId (FK) and userId (owner FK)
- New priceRange field (1=Budget, 2=Moderate, 3=Upscale)
- Address information is no longer stored directly on Restaurant
- Users must be created first, then Addresses, then Restaurants

📋 RECOMMENDED TEST ORDER:
1. Get or Create User (required for owner)
2. Create Address (required for restaurant location)
3. Create Restaurant (with userId and addressId)
4. Read/Update/Delete Restaurant tests

Base URL: http://localhost:8080
API Version: v1 (/api/v1)

item.name: 0. SETUP - Get Existing User (or create if needed)

request.description:
Retrieve list of existing users.

📌 You MUST have at least one user before creating a restaurant!

Response will include user IDs. Copy a user ID for use in the next steps.

If no users exist, you'll need to:
1. Use 'POST - Create User' request below
2. Note the returned user ID
3. Use that ID in restaurant creation

Create a new user account (restaurant owner).

✅ REQUIRED: You must create a user before creating restaurants

Response will include:
- id: Use this ID in restaurant creation (as userId)
- email: Unique identifier

Note the returned user ID for use in address and restaurant creation.

query.description: Owner's user ID (from user creation above)

request.description:
Create an address entity.

✅ REQUIRED: Schema v2.0 - Restaurants must reference an Address via addressId FK

Query Parameter:
- userId: The ID of the user creating this address (use the user ID from previous step)

Response will include:
- id: Use this ID in restaurant creation (as addressId)

⚠️ Replace '1' in the userId parameter with the actual user ID you created

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Burger King",
  "email": "contact@burgerking.com",
  "phoneNumber": "(555) 123-4567",
  "description": "Premium fast food restaurant chain",
  "priceRange": 1,
  "isActive": true
}

query.description: Owner user ID (from GET users step)

request.description:
Create a new restaurant (Schema v2.0).

📌 SCHEMA v2.0 REQUIREMENTS:
- Query parameter 'userId': The restaurant owner (required FK)
- Query parameter 'addressId': The restaurant location (required FK)
- priceRange: 1=Budget, 2=Moderate, 3=Upscale (required)
- name, email, phoneNumber: Basic info (required)

Expected Response: HTTP 201 Created

⚠️ Replace userId=1 and addressId=1 with actual IDs from setup steps

Response will include the created restaurant with auto-generated ID

Create a second restaurant to have multiple records for testing.

⚠️ For v2.0: Specify priceRange (2=Moderate)

Create a second address (optional - for creating restaurants in different locations).

Note the returned address ID to use in additional restaurant creation requests.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Burger King",
  "email": "contact@burgerking.com",
  "phoneNumber": "(555) 123-4567",
  "street": "789 Fast Food Blvd",
  "city": "Metropolis",
  "state": "NY",
  "zipCode": "10001",
  "country": "USA",
  "description": "Fast food restaurant chain",
  "isActive": true
}

request.description:
Create a new restaurant in the database.

Expected Response: HTTP 201 Created
Response will include the created restaurant with auto-generated ID

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Pizza Palace",
  "email": "info@pizzapalace.com",
  "phoneNumber": "(555) 567-8901",
  "street": "123 Main Street",
  "city": "Springfield",
  "state": "IL",
  "zipCode": "62701",
  "country": "USA",
  "description": "Authentic Italian pizza restaurant",
  "isActive": true
}

request.description: Create a second restaurant to have multiple records for testing.

Retrieve all restaurants via Web UI.

⚠️ NOTE: The REST API GET /api/v1/restaurants has a 500 error due to circular reference issues with v2.0 FKs.

WORKAROUND: Use the Back Office Web UI endpoint instead:
- GET /backoffice/restaurants — Works perfectly (200 OK)

Expected Response: HTTP 200 OK

For API development, use single-entity endpoints:
- GET /api/v1/restaurants/{id} — Get specific restaurant (works fine)

Retrieve a specific restaurant by its ID.

✅ Schema v2.0 response will include:
- id, name, email, phoneNumber
- priceRange: 1, 2, or 3
- user: { id, firstName, lastName, email } (owner FK)
- address: { id, street, city, state, zipCode, country } (address FK)
- isActive, createdAt, updatedAt

Expected Response: HTTP 200 OK

Note: Replace '1' with an actual restaurant ID from the CREATE response

Test 404 error response when restaurant doesn't exist.

Expected Response: HTTP 404 Not Found with error message

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Burger King Premium",
  "email": "premium@burgerking.com",
  "phoneNumber": "(555) 999-9999",
  "description": "Premium fast food restaurant chain",
  "priceRange": 2,
  "isActive": true
}

request.description:
Update an existing restaurant's information.

📌 Schema v2.0 Changes:
- NO address fields (street, city, etc) in body anymore
- Only update restaurant-specific fields
- Owner ID passed as query parameter
- To change address: use separate Address API

Expected Response: HTTP 200 OK with updated restaurant

Note: Replace '1' in path and userId with actual values

Delete a restaurant from the database.

Schema v2.0: Requires userId parameter (authorization check)

Expected Response: HTTP 204 No Content

Note: Replace '2' with the restaurant ID you want to delete

Verify that the deleted restaurant no longer exists.

Expected Response: HTTP 404 Not Found

Note: Use the ID of the restaurant you just deleted

item.name: 6. VERIFY - Get All Restaurants After Delete

request.description:
Verify the restaurant count has decreased after deletion.

Use Web UI endpoint (works better than REST API list endpoint)

Expected Response: HTTP 200 OK with one fewer restaurant

Test validation error handling.

Expected Response: HTTP 400 Bad Request with validation error messages

Test 404 error when updating non-existent restaurant.

Expected Response: HTTP 404 Not Found with error message

Test 404 error when deleting non-existent restaurant.

Expected Response: HTTP 404 Not Found with error message
